title: Alnylam — AHP — briefing Q3-2025
html:
GIVLAARI / AHP Briefing: Q3 2025

Period covered: July - September 2025 Key data sources: Global ATU Wave 4 readout (fielded Sep-Oct 2024, delivered early 2025), Alnylam Q2 2025 earnings (July 31, 2025), ongoing ZoomRx engagement

Headline

GIVLAARI's global position solidifies as the ATU Wave 4 data confirms a critical disease perception shift toward AHP chronicity -- but revenue growth is governed by new patient identification, not competitive dynamics. The data reveals a clear HCP adoption ladder with different barriers at each rung, and Brazil remains structurally stalled.

1. Commercial Performance

  • Alnylam Q2 2025 earnings (July 31): Rare disease portfolio (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) continues growth trajectory. GIVLAARI patient growth ~15% YoY driven by new patient starts
  • Revenue context: GIVLAARI is a stable, mature franchise within Alnylam. The company's aggressive growth targets (~25% CAGR to 2030) are anchored on the TTR franchise (AMVUTTRA), not rare disease
  • Pricing: Annual treatment cost remains ~$442K after discounts ($575K list). Prevalence-based value agreements in place with select payers

What Alnylam management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. "GIVLAARI continues to serve the AHP community as the only approved prophylactic therapy" — management positioned GIVLAARI as a franchise with no competitive threat, reinforcing the uncontested market narrative.
  2. "GIVLAARI is a stable franchise contributing to our base" — explicitly categorizing AHP as base business, not a growth driver. This confirms GIVLAARI's role as a predictable revenue contributor while TTR captures management's growth ambitions.
  3. AHP received minimal management airtime relative to the ATTR franchise — consistent with the thesis that GIVLAARI is a "maintain and grow" product, not a strategic priority.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. No analyst questions on AHP or GIVLAARI during Q&A — the analyst community has fully accepted AHP as a stable, uncontested franchise and does not view it as a source of upside or risk.
  2. The absence of questions itself is the signal: GIVLAARI is priced for steady-state growth, not catalysts.

2. Data Readouts This Quarter

Global ATU Wave 4 Full Report (Delivered ~Q1-Q2 2025, from Sep-Oct 2024 fielding)

This is the richest data package ZoomRx has delivered for GIVLAARI. Key findings that should shape all client conversations:

Disease Perception Shift (Cross-Market):

  • US: Majority of HCPs now view AHP as predominantly chronic. Primary objective shifted to preventing long-term complications from elevated ALA/PBG -- statistically significant shift
  • Germany: Balanced approach (attacks + LTC) -- the "equilibrium" state
  • Brazil: Still attack-focused. HCPs less likely to consider non-medical-intervention events as true attacks

GIVLAARI Usage Trends:

  • US: GIVLAARI usage modestly increased (+3%), PANHEMATIN as-needed dropped (-6%). Expected growth: +8% in next 12 months from prophylactic PANHEMATIN conversion and Tx-naive starts
  • Germany: Biggest share growth (+14%). Already highest GIVLAARI share globally. Expected further +9% growth
  • Brazil: Share modestly decreased (-6%). Expected flat/modest increase. ~45% of diagnosed patients not on consistent treatment due to cost

HCP Segmentation (US):

  • Enthusiasts (11%): High load, high share. Need payer support
  • Trialists (21%): Low load, high share. Need OOP cost relief
  • Converters (22%): High load, low share. Need efficacy differentiation vs. hemin
  • Inexperienced (47%): Low load, low share. Need education + payer pathway. Largest opportunity

Barriers (Persistent):

  • US/Brazil: OOP costs + payer coverage top 2 barriers
  • Germany: Patient refusal + administration burden. Payer barriers decreasing
  • Attack-count inclusion criteria create a Catch-22 across all markets

3. Competitive Landscape

  • No new competitive entrants. AHP pipeline remains sparse. No novel mechanism approaching late-stage development
  • PANHEMATIN/NORMOSANG: FDA designated PANHEMATIN an "essential medicine" -- securing long-term supply but not expanding its role vs. GIVLAARI
  • The real competition remains non-diagnosis and non-treatment

Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q3 2025

GIVLAARI operates in an effectively uncontested market. There is no direct prophylactic competitor, and the "competitive" landscape is better described as a patient-finding challenge than a market share battle.

Recordati — Panhematin (hemin): Recordati provides limited earnings disclosure for Panhematin. Estimated annual revenue is ~$30-40M, reflecting its role as an acute treatment for AHP attacks rather than a prophylactic therapy. Panhematin and GIVLAARI are largely complementary (Panhematin for acute attacks, GIVLAARI for prevention), and the FDA's "essential medicine" designation secures long-term hemin supply without expanding its commercial role. Recordati's rare disease portfolio earnings commentary does not single out Panhematin as a growth driver.

Competitive reality: GIVLAARI's true competition is (1) undiagnosed patients (the 15-year average diagnostic delay), (2) hemin for acute management in patients not yet converted to prophylaxis, and (3) liver transplant as the curative option for the most severe cases. No novel mechanism is approaching late-stage clinical development for AHP. GIVLAARI's first-in-class monopoly remains unchallenged through 2027+, and commercial growth is governed entirely by the rate of new patient identification.

4. Strategic Implications for ZoomRx

  • The ATU Wave 4 data needs to inform a PET refresh. The most recent PET (Q2 2023) predates the chronicity narrative shift by 15+ months
  • Alnylam will likely push for deeper Patient Type 4/5 exploration in future ATU waves
  • Brazil should be flagged as requiring a differentiated research approach -- possibly a dedicated access study
  • HCP segmentation framework (4 segments) should become the standard lens for all GIVLAARI analysis

5. What Vinoth Should Know

  • GIVLAARI/AHP is a stable, recurring engagement. Not high-drama but consistent revenue
  • The brand's commercial challenge is uniquely "rare disease" -- finding patients, not beating competitors
  • The cross-market heterogeneity (US/Germany/Brazil) makes this a good showcase for ZoomRx's global capabilities
  • The ATU Wave 4 was an exceptionally rich deliverable. If there are client conversations about expanding scope (diagnostic journey study, access study), this quarter's data provides the justification
